summary: Guard against ExpiresIn==0 on initial token storage in auth.go (parallel guard already exists in refreshAccessToken).
reason: auth.go:167 set `expiry := time.Now().Add(time.Duration(tokenResp.ExpiresIn) * time.Second)` unconditionally. If the OIDC server returns expires_in=0 (non-conformant but possible), expiry == time.Now() (already past) → next request triggers an unnecessary refresh round-trip.
validated_outcome: Mirrors the existing client.go:418 guard: `expiry := time.Time{}; if tokenResp.ExpiresIn > 0 { expiry = time.Now().Add(...) }`. Eliminates the spurious refresh per call when ExpiresIn is 0.
